product_overview: Backend APIs to support an ISP management dashboard for authentication, router management, client and package lifecycle, hotspot purchases, transactions and voucher handling. Provides REST endpoints used by the Next.js frontend to power dashboards, admin configuration and hotspot integration.
core_goals: Provide secure authentication and session verification for admin and system users via JWT.
Expose CRUD APIs for clients, routers, packages, subscriptions, transactions and vouchers to support the admin UI.
Integrate hotspot purchase and callback flows for voucher and subscription creation.
Deliver a dashboard summary endpoint that aggregates revenue, router status and usage metrics for the UI.
Ensure clear error handling and validation on all endpoints so the frontend can surface actionable messages.

features.name: Authentication
features.description: Register and sign in users; issue and verify JWTs. Endpoints: /api/auth/register, /api/auth/login, /api/auth/me. (See login/register pages in PRD visuals.)
user_flows: Navigate to /api/auth/register -> Fill registration payload (name, email, password) -> POST /api/auth/register -> Receive 201 Created with JWT -> Store token locally -> Call GET /api/auth/me with Authorization header -> See current user profile
Navigate to /api/auth/login -> Fill email field -> Fill password field -> POST /api/auth/login -> Receive 401 Unauthorized -> See error message 'Invalid credentials' from response

features.name: Dashboard Summary
features.description: Provide aggregated metrics and charts for the admin UI (revenue, router status, active users, recent transactions). Backend endpoint: /api/dashboard. (Dashboard layout and widgets visible on PRD pages 1 and 9.)
user_flows: Call GET /api/dashboard with valid Authorization header -> Receive 200 OK -> See revenue, router status and active users data in response
Call GET /api/dashboard without Authorization header -> Receive 401 Unauthorized -> UI shows login prompt or 'Not authenticated' error

features.name: Client Management
features.description: List, create and manage customers (PPPoE and Hotspot) via /api/clients and /api/clients/[id]. Supports status filters (Active, Banned, Suspended) as shown in the PRD client management page.
user_flows: Call GET /api/clients with Authorization header -> Receive 200 OK -> See list of clients with status and plan fields
Navigate to /api/clients -> Fill client payload (username, fullName, phone, planId) -> POST /api/clients -> Receive 201 Created -> Call GET /api/clients -> See newly created client in list
Navigate to /api/clients -> Fill incomplete client payload (missing required field) -> POST /api/clients -> Receive 400 Bad Request -> See validation error for the missing field

features.name: Router Management
features.description: Add, list and manage MikroTik routers via /api/routers. Includes router setup workflow, VPN configuration, and downloadable setup files (PRD pages 7-11 describe the setup wizard and downloadable scripts).
user_flows: Navigate to /api/routers -> Fill router data (accessCode, name, vpnMode, description, initialStatus) -> POST /api/routers -> Receive 201 Created -> Response includes setup instructions and download links
Navigate to /api/routers -> Fill router name with invalid characters -> POST /api/routers -> Receive 400 Bad Request -> See validation error 'Router Name invalid format'

features.name: Packages Management
features.description: Create and list service packages (Hotspot, PPPoE, Static IP) including bandwidth, duration, router assignment and hotspot settings via /api/packages. The PRD shows the package creation UI with bandwidth, duration and hotspot options (pages 3-4).
user_flows: Navigate to /api/packages -> Fill package payload (type, name, price, duration, routerId, uploadSpeed, downloadSpeed, hotspotSettings) -> POST /api/packages -> Receive 201 Created -> Call GET /api/packages -> See new package in list
Navigate to /api/packages -> Fill package payload with non-numeric bandwidth -> POST /api/packages -> Receive 400 Bad Request -> See field error 'uploadSpeed/downloadSpeed must be numeric'

features.name: Subscriptions
features.description: Create and list subscriptions when customers purchase packages; handle expiry and extensions using /api/subscriptions. Subscriptions power active/expired views in the client UI.
user_flows: Navigate to /api/subscriptions -> Fill subscription payload (userId, packageId, startDate) -> POST /api/subscriptions -> Receive 201 Created -> Call GET /api/subscriptions -> See active subscription
Navigate to /api/subscriptions -> POST subscription with packageId for a disabled/removed package -> Receive 409 Conflict -> See error 'Package not available'

features.name: Transactions
features.description: List and create payment transactions (M-Pesa, Paybill, manual) via /api/transactions. The PRD includes transaction logs, filters and export capabilities (pages 5-6, 12-16 describe channels and logs).
user_flows: Call GET /api/transactions with Authorization header -> Receive 200 OK -> See paginated transaction records with user, plan, amount and method
Navigate to /api/transactions -> Fill transaction payload (username, planId, amount, method) -> POST /api/transactions -> Receive 201 Created -> See transaction in GET /api/transactions results
Navigate to /api/transactions -> Fill transaction payload with negative amount -> POST /api/transactions -> Receive 400 Bad Request -> See error 'Amount must be positive'

features.name: Vouchers
features.description: Generate, list, print and delete vouchers via /api/vouchers. Supports bulk operations and filtering by router as shown on the PRD vouchers page (pages 5-6).
user_flows: Navigate to /api/vouchers -> Fill voucher generation payload (type, routerId, planId, quantity) -> POST /api/vouchers -> Receive 201 Created with voucher codes -> UI shows printable voucher list

Navigate to /api/vouchers -> Select vouchers and click delete all used -> DELETE /api/vouchers with filter used=true -> Receive 400 Bad Request when no vouchers selected -> See error 'No vouchers selected'

features.name: Hotspot Integration
features.description: Hotspot purchase and callback handling for direct purchases and external hotspot systems. Endpoints include /api/hotspot/purchase and hotspot callback handlers (files referenced in the codebase). Used by captive portals to issue vouchers or activate subscriptions.
user_flows: Navigate to /api/hotspot/purchase -> Fill purchase payload (username, routerId, packageId, paymentInfo) -> POST /api/hotspot/purchase -> Receive 200 OK with voucher or activation details -> Client receives access credentials
Receive POST at /api/hotspot/callback from external hotspot -> Validate callback payload and signature -> Update subscription/voucher state -> Respond 200 OK to caller

Navigate to /api/hotspot/purchase -> Fill purchase payload with insufficient payment details -> POST /api/hotspot/purchase -> Receive 402 Payment Required or 400 -> See error 'Payment failed or insufficient funds'

routes.description: User login with username and password
Get, update, and delete a client

features.name: Authentication
features.description: User login, registration, and token verification
